macro_context.market_sentiment: Mixed-to-cautious. Dow fell 280 pts (-0.57%) for a fifth straight losing day; S&P 500 flat at 7,136; Nasdaq barely positive. Markets await Magnificent Seven earnings (Alphabet, Microsoft, Amazon, Meta reporting after bell) while managing oil price shock and Fed uncertainty.
macro_context.fed_outlook: Fed held benchmark rate at 3.5%-3.75% (third consecutive hold) with a historic 8-4 dissent — the widest split since October 1992. Inflation remains above 2% target (March CPI 3.3% YoY) driven by energy prices and tariffs. No rate cuts expected near-term; May be Powell's final meeting as Fed Chair.
macro_context.geopolitical: Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz in March 2026, disrupting ~27% of global maritime oil trade. Brent crude surpassed $100/bbl for the first time since August 2022. U.S.-Iran talks have stalled; Trump reportedly preparing an extended naval blockade. IMF cut 2026 global growth to 3.1% from 3.4%. Tariffs and energy price shock are the dominant macro headwinds.
key_events: Magnificent Seven earnings (Alphabet, Microsoft, Amazon, Meta) reporting April 29 after close — major sentiment event
Fed rate hold confirmed April 29 — no near-term cut; leadership transition risk with Powell potentially replaced
U.S.-Iran negotiations stalled — oil supply disruption may persist, keeping energy costs elevated
IMF downgraded global growth forecast to 3.1% — consumer spending headwinds for discretionary brands like Nike
Consumer sentiment under pressure from $4+ gas prices and tariff-driven inflation

ticker_news.headline: Nike cuts 1,400 jobs in second round of tech and operations layoffs in 2026, consolidating to Oregon and India hubs
NKE short interest jumped 22.8% in April to ~56.1 million shares, reflecting growing bearish positioning
Nike Q3 FY2026 results: EPS $0.35 beat estimates ($0.28), revenue $11.28B — fourth consecutive quarterly beat
Nike dropped 3.1% on April 7 after CICC downgrade, setting a new 52-week low; Jim Cramer called it a 'damning' downgrade
Nike Wholesale rose 5% to $6.5B in Q3 while Nike Direct slipped 4% and digital fell 9%; Converse dropped 35%

analyst_consensus.recent_changes: Wave of April 2026 downgrades: CICC cut to market perform (PT $58 from $69), DA Davidson cut to neutral (PT $46 from $72), UBS neutral (PT $54 from $58), Wells Fargo maintained overweight but cut PT to $55 from $65, Stifel set hold at $56. Barclays bucked the trend with an upgrade to Overweight in March (PT $73). Split consensus: 17 Buy, 17 Hold, 2 Sell per GuruFocus as of April 29, 2026. 3-month trailing avg PT $60.90.

web_context.competitor_activity: Under Armour's footwear business is in freefall, restructuring aggressively (cut Steph Curry endorsement deal for $45-50M in savings) — cedes share to Nike in performance. Adidas executing a rapid turnaround on Terrace shoe trend (Samba, Gazelle), posting robust growth especially in Europe and Latin America — directly competitive in lifestyle segment. Hoka and On Holding remain high-momentum challengers with strong recent earnings in running/performance. Kylian Mbappe's Nike deal expires July 31, 2026; Adidas and Under Armour are both aggressively pursuing him, which could be a brand/marketing blow to Nike if lost.
web_context.earnings_info: Next earnings: June 25, 2026 (after market close, confirmed — Q4 FY2026). Q4 EPS estimate: $0.12; Q4 revenue estimate: $10.86B. Most recent report: Q3 FY2026 on March 31, 2026 — EPS $0.35 beat (estimate $0.28), revenue $11.28B (fourth consecutive beat). Sources: MarketBeat, Nasdaq, Nike Investor Relations.
